root_null.name: seg_then_root_concat
root_null.description: When a segment's end is the start of a root, treat the segment's start as a virtual root over the concatenation. Models the 'peeled tail' invariant idiom (lseg(p,q) * listrep(q)).

segment_eq.name: seg_eq_forward_rooted
segment_eq.description: Segment-emptiness for `start != end` / `start == end`. `sllseg(x,y,l)` alone does NOT give `x = y <=> l = []` (a lasso — a cycle from x back to y with l <> [] — is satisfiable). A root `sll(y,_)` terminating the segment's END is the acyclicity witness that makes the equivalence sound. This rule matches the queried endpoints as (start=$x, end=$y) and REQUIRES that trailing root; the emit is the segment's emptiness.

segment_eq.name: seg_eq_reversed_rooted
segment_eq.description: Same as seg_eq_forward_rooted with the comparison operands written in the opposite order (the segment is (start=$y, end=$x)). Segment emptiness is symmetric so the emit is identical; the required acyclicity root still sits at the segment's real END ($S.end).
